q: Carbon is the main constituent of fossil fuels.
Chemical energy stored in fossil fuels is converted into heat, light, and mechanical energy.
The fossil fuel industry extracts crude oil from the ground and converts it into gasoline.
Petrochemical products include plastics and polyurethane.
The process of coal formation is called coalification.
Coal is made of decomposed plant matter in conditions of high temperature and pressure.
Coal is a uniform substance with a constant composition from deposit to deposit.
Methane is a common gas found in swamps.
Nuclear energy is renewable because uranium is found in the Earth's crust.
Uranium fuels are consumed in the nuclear fission reaction.
Vessels driven by nuclear energy can sail for long periods without having to refuel.
Radioisotopes obtained as by-products in nuclear reactions are used in medicine and agriculture.
